project.name: dentalemon
project.description: Dentalemon — full-stack dental clinic platform. Merged per-domain knowledge graph: frontend (apps/dentalemon, React+Vite+TanStack) + backend (services/api-ts, Hono+Drizzle+Postgres). Built per UA_KG_UPGRADE (per-domain scoping, U1 FE + BE arm). See domain-graph.json for the FE↔BE business-flow layer.

nodes.name: perio-comparison.tsx
nodes.summary: Longitudinal perio comparison view rendering current-vs-history trend rows and per-tooth probing-depth deltas, driven by perio-history data and comparison logic.

nodes.name: PerioComparisonView
nodes.summary: Presentational component rendering the summary trend table and per-tooth comparison grid with delta badges.

nodes.name: PerioComparison
nodes.summary: Container that loads perio history and feeds comparison logic into the presentational view.

nodes.name: perio-site-cell.tsx
nodes.summary: Editable cell for a single perio measurement site (probing depth / gingival margin) with threshold-based highlighting.

nodes.name: PerioSiteCell
nodes.summary: Renders an editable per-site measurement cell, highlighting over-threshold values and emitting changes.

nodes.name: perio-summary-bar.tsx
nodes.summary: Summary bar showing live perio metrics (BOP%, mean depth, deep-pocket count) for the in-progress exam.

nodes.name: PerioSummaryBar
nodes.summary: Renders aggregate perio metric tiles for the current charting session.

nodes.name: perio-tooth-column.tsx
nodes.summary: Per-tooth column in the perio grid stacking site cells, BOP dots, and CAL cells for buccal/lingual surfaces with a missing-tooth toggle.

nodes.name: PerioToothColumn
nodes.summary: Renders all measurement sites for one tooth, composing site cells, BOP dots, and CAL cells across surfaces.

nodes.name: perio-types.ts
nodes.summary: Core perio domain types, constants (FDI teeth, site definitions, thresholds), and pure helpers for measurements, summaries, and charting sequence navigation.
nodes.languageNotes: Single source of truth for perio domain model — types, FDI tooth tables, threshold constants, and pure compute helpers.

nodes.name: computeLivePerioSummary
nodes.summary: Computes live perio summary metrics (BOP%, mean depth, deep-pocket count) from current readings.

nodes.name: countOverThreshold
nodes.summary: Counts measurement sites whose probing depth exceeds the configured threshold.

nodes.name: buildPerioSequence
nodes.summary: Builds the ordered sequence of charting steps (tooth/site/field) for guided/voice data entry.

nodes.name: perio-voice-grammar.ts
nodes.summary: Voice grammar for perio charting: normalizes transcripts, resolves homophones, and parses spoken utterances into structured depth/margin/grade/command tokens.
nodes.languageNotes: Includes a homophone table and transcript normalization tuned for clinical dictation accuracy.

nodes.name: parseUtterance
nodes.summary: Parses a normalized voice transcript into structured perio measurement values and navigation commands.

nodes.name: normalizeTranscript
nodes.summary: Normalizes raw speech transcript text (casing, homophones, fillers) before parsing.

nodes.name: perio-voice-sequencer.ts
nodes.summary: State machine that advances the perio charting cursor through the measurement sequence as parsed voice writes arrive, skipping missing teeth.

nodes.name: advance
nodes.summary: Applies a parsed voice write to sequencer state and advances the cursor, handling navigation commands and missing teeth.

nodes.name: initSequencerState
nodes.summary: Initializes the voice sequencer state at the first chartable step.

nodes.name: speech-provider.ts
nodes.summary: Speech-recognition abstraction with a Web Speech API implementation and a fake provider for testing, plus support detection and perio grammar hints.
nodes.languageNotes: Provider interface with real (WebSpeech) and fake implementations enables deterministic testing of voice flows.

nodes.name: WebSpeechProvider
nodes.summary: Speech provider backed by the browser Web Speech API, emitting transcript results and lifecycle events.

nodes.name: FakeSpeechProvider
nodes.summary: Deterministic in-memory speech provider used for tests and environments without speech support.

nodes.name: PerioApiError

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Two-stage Docker build for the dentalemon SPA: a Bun builder stage that installs the full workspace, compiles the Vite app, and a Caddy alpine stage that serves the resulting dist via a runtime-configurable entrypoint.
nodes.languageNotes: Multi-stage build copies every workspace package.json before `bun install --frozen-lockfile` so the lockfile resolves the whole workspace graph; runtime config (PORT, API_URL, ONESIGNAL_APP_ID) is injected via env vars consumed by docker-entrypoint.sh before Caddy starts.

nodes.name: builder
nodes.summary: Bun-based build stage (oven/bun:1) that copies all workspace manifests, installs frozen dependencies, copies sources, and runs `bun run build` to produce the dentalemon Vite dist output.

nodes.name: caddy
nodes.summary: Production stage (caddy:2.8-alpine) that serves the built SPA from /srv on port 80, using the project Caddyfile and an entrypoint script that generates config.json at runtime.

nodes.name: .dockerignore
nodes.summary: Whitelist-style Docker build-context filter that ignores everything by default and re-includes only the dist output, docker-entrypoint.sh, Caddyfile, and Dockerfile needed for the image build.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Template of environment variables for the Vite frontend, documenting the API base URL and OneSignal push-notification app ID/tag consumed at build/runtime.
nodes.languageNotes: VITE_-prefixed vars are inlined into the client bundle by Vite at build time.

nodes.summary: Developer guide for the dentalemon app covering app overview, tech stack, directory structure, routes, and the development workflow with a command quick reference.

nodes.name: bunfig.toml

nodes.name: docker-entrypoint.sh
nodes.summary: Container entrypoint that generates a runtime /tmp/config.json from API_URL and ONESIGNAL_APP_ID env vars (writable under read-only K8s) before exec'ing Caddy to serve the static SPA.
nodes.languageNotes: Writes config to /tmp so the app can be served from a read-only filesystem; runtime env injection avoids rebuilding the bundle per environment.

nodes.name: index.html

nodes.name: package.json

nodes.name: docker-compose.deps.yml

nodes.name: postgres

nodes.name: stripe-mock

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Multi-stage Bun (alpine) build: a spec-builder stage compiles the TypeSpec/OpenAPI artifacts, then the runtime stage installs api-ts dependencies, copies source, exposes port 7213, and runs the server with a wget healthcheck.
nodes.languageNotes: Two-stage build with BuildKit cache mounts for the Bun install cache; the spec-builder stage generates specs/api/dist which is copied into the runtime image so the API can import generated OpenAPI/types at startup.

nodes.name: expand.ts
nodes.summary: Hono middleware that intercepts JSON responses and applies query-driven resource expansion (?expand=) by delegating to the expand utility's applyExpands.

nodes.name: validation.ts
nodes.summary: Provides a shared zValidator error hook that converts Zod validation failures into the canonical API error envelope with field and global error details.

nodes.name: expand.ts
nodes.summary: Core resource-expansion engine that recursively resolves ?expand= paths against the OpenAPI schema, fetching related records via internal route calls up to a bounded depth.
nodes.languageNotes: Recursive depth-bounded traversal driven by OpenAPI schema refs (anyOf/$ref) with Promise.all fan-out for array fields.

nodes.name: createExpandMiddleware
nodes.summary: Factory returning Hono middleware that, after the handler runs, clones the JSON response, parses the expand query param, and rewrites the body with expanded resources.

nodes.name: validationErrorHandler
nodes.summary: zValidator hook that collects Zod issues into field and global error arrays and returns a 400 JSON error envelope with a correlation request id.

nodes.name: transformExpandResponse
nodes.summary: Entry point that reads the expand query param, clones and parses the JSON response, and applies expansions before returning a rewritten Response.

nodes.name: applyExpands
nodes.summary: Recursively walks data (objects, arrays, paginated envelopes) and expands grouped paths against the schema's expandable fields up to the depth limit.

nodes.name: getExpandableFields
nodes.summary: Inspects an OpenAPI schema's properties and returns the names of fields marked as expandable.

nodes.name: findRouteByOperationId
nodes.summary: Looks up a route's method and path by its OpenAPI operationId so expansion can issue an internal fetch to the target resource.

nodes.name: expandField
nodes.summary: Resolves the target schema ref for a single expandable field and dispatches to array or single expansion, recursing into remaining nested expand paths.

nodes.name: expandSingleField
nodes.summary: Expands a single id-valued field by issuing an internal app.request to the resolved route and recursively applying any remaining expand paths.

nodes.name: expandArrayField
nodes.summary: Expands an array of id values by fanning out parallel single-field expansions via Promise.all, filtering nullish entries first.

nodes.name: groupExpandsByFirstSegment
nodes.summary: Groups dotted expand paths by their first segment so nested expansions on the same field are processed together.

nodes.summary: React component rendering selectable reminder offset-day options, deduping and sorting the selection before saving to branch settings.

nodes.name: useBranchSettings

nodes.name: useUpdateBranchSettings

nodes.name: formatCurrency

nodes.name: makeEntry
nodes.summary: Test fixture factory that builds an audit-log entry object with sensible defaults, allowing per-test field overrides.

nodes.name: addPatientCredit.ts
nodes.summary: Hono handler that records a patient credit (BR-052) against an active membership, resolving the patient's billing branch, enforcing branch access, and writing an audit event.

nodes.name: addPatientCredit
nodes.summary: Validates the credit request, resolves the patient's billing branch and active membership, persists the credit via DentalPatientCreditRepository, logs an audit event, and returns the created credit.

nodes.name: applyCreditToInvoice.ts
nodes.summary: Hono handler that applies a patient credit to an invoice inside an atomic tenant-scoped transaction, re-reading balances under a row lock to prevent over-application, then auditing the movement.

nodes.name: applyCreditToInvoice
nodes.summary: Loads the target invoice, enforces branch access, then within withTenantTx locks the invoice row, re-validates credit balance, records the credit application as a payment, and writes a ledger entry plus audit event.

nodes.name: createCollectionNote.ts
nodes.summary: Hono handler that logs a collections contact note (BR-051) against a patient's billing branch and writes an accompanying audit event.

nodes.name: createCollectionNote
nodes.summary: Resolves the patient's billing branch, enforces branch access and active membership, persists the collection note via DentalCollectionNoteRepository, logs an audit event, and returns the serialized note.

nodes.name: createDentalInvoice.ts
nodes.summary: Hono handler that generates a dental invoice from a visit's billable treatments inside an atomic transaction, enforcing signed-consent and role gates, marking treatments billed, and emitting an invoice-created notification.

nodes.name: createDentalInvoice
nodes.summary: Validates role, checks idempotency by localId, requires signed consent for the visit, aggregates billable treatments into line items with tax, creates the invoice and line items, marks treatments billed, audits, and fires emitInvoiceCreated.

nodes.name: dental-billing.collections-worklist.test.ts

nodes.summary: Comprehensive integration test suite covering the invoice lifecycle (issue, pay, void/refund) with seeded invoices and payments, including adversarial money-movement cases.

nodes.summary: Handler returning a patient's credit ledger entries and current credit balance after enforcing patient-branch access.

nodes.name: dunning.ts
nodes.summary: Scheduled dunning sweep that finds overdue invoices, claims per-branch reminder offsets idempotently, and dispatches payment-reminder notifications, releasing or recording outcome per invoice.

nodes.summary: Selects outstanding overdue invoices, resolves per-branch reminder offsets, claims reminder slots idempotently, sends payment-reminder notifications, and records send outcomes.

nodes.name: dental-billing-reminder-log.repo.ts
nodes.summary: Repository for the dunning reminder-log table; atomically claims a reminder slot per invoice/offset-day (insert-on-conflict-do-nothing), releases it, and records the send outcome (channel/status).
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Drizzle onConflictDoNothing on (invoiceId, offsetDay) as a concurrency claim primitive so scheduled dunning runs cannot double-send.

nodes.name: DentalBillingReminderLogRepository
nodes.summary: Drizzle repository exposing claim/release/setOutcome over the dunning reminder-log table, providing idempotent reminder dispatch bookkeeping.

nodes.name: dental-billing-reminder-log.schema.ts
nodes.summary: Drizzle table definition for the dunning reminder log, keyed by invoice and offset-day to support idempotent reminder scheduling.

nodes.name: dentalBillingReminderLog
nodes.summary: Reminder-log table tracking dunning reminders per invoice/offset-day with channel and status fields.

nodes.name: dental-collection-note.repo.ts

nodes.name: DentalCollectionNoteRepository
nodes.summary: Drizzle repository over collection notes with create, listByPatient, and findLatestByPatients (batch latest-contact lookup via inArray).

nodes.name: dental-collection-note.schema.ts
nodes.summary: Drizzle table definition for collection notes recording patient contact attempts (channel, contacted timestamp) in the collections worklist.

nodes.name: dentalCollectionNotes

nodes.name: dental-invoice.repo.ts
nodes.summary: Core invoice repository handling invoice and line-item lifecycle: create, issue, void, mark uncollectible, apply payments/discounts, recalculate totals/tax, and batch-mark overdue invoices.
nodes.languageNotes: Money mutations (addPayment/removePayment) use raw Drizzle sql`` CASE expressions with GREATEST() to clamp balances and derive status atomically inside a single UPDATE, avoiding read-modify-write races.

nodes.name: DentalInvoiceRepository
nodes.summary: Drizzle repository for invoices and line items with 18 methods covering creation, status transitions, payment application, discount/tax recalculation, and overdue sweeping.

nodes.name: dental-invoice.schema.ts
nodes.summary: Drizzle schema defining the invoice status enum, the invoices table (cents-based totals/paid/balance, status timestamps), and the invoice line-items table.

nodes.name: dentalInvoices
nodes.summary: Invoices table with cents-based subtotal/tax/discount/total/paid/balance columns and a status enum spanning draft through paid/voided/uncollectible/overdue.

nodes.name: dentalInvoiceLineItems
nodes.summary: Invoice line-items table holding per-item CDT codes and amounts associated with a parent invoice.

nodes.name: dental-patient-credit.repo.ts
nodes.summary: Repository for the patient credit ledger: appends credit entries, lists them per patient, and computes a patient's running credit balance via SUM aggregation.

nodes.name: DentalPatientCreditRepository
nodes.summary: Drizzle repository over the patient credit ledger with create, listByPatient, and getBalance (COALESCE SUM) operations.

nodes.name: dental-patient-credit.schema.ts
nodes.summary: Drizzle table definition for the patient credit ledger storing signed cents amounts per patient credit/debit entry.

nodes.name: dentalPatientCredits
nodes.summary: Patient-credit ledger table recording cents-denominated credit entries summed to derive a patient's available balance.

nodes.name: dental-payment-refund.repo.ts
nodes.summary: Repository for payment refunds: records a refund against a payment, lists refunds for a payment, and totals refunded amount to guard against over-refunding.

nodes.name: DentalPaymentRefundRepository
nodes.summary: Drizzle repository over payment refunds with create, listByPayment, and totalRefundedForPayment (COALESCE SUM) for refund-cap enforcement.

nodes.name: dental-payment-refund.schema.ts
nodes.summary: Drizzle table definition for payment refunds linking refunded cents amounts back to their originating payment.

nodes.name: dentalPaymentRefunds
nodes.summary: Payment-refunds table recording cents-denominated refunds against payments, summed to enforce refund caps.

nodes.name: sendPatientStatement.test.ts
nodes.summary: DB-backed integration test for the patient-statement endpoint, seeding invoices and asserting that a billing notification is created for the outstanding balance.

nodes.name: payment-terms.test.ts

nodes.name: payment-terms.ts

nodes.name: erasure-engine.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the data-erasure engine, building erasure targets and a spying audit sink to verify per-entity erasure events are emitted.

layers.name: [FE] Routing & App Shell
layers.description: TanStack file-based routes, the router/app bootstrap, and shared app-level UI components (sidebar, dialogs, empty/error states) that frame every screen.

layers.name: [FE] Feature Modules
layers.description: Vertical product slices (workspace, imaging, billing, scheduling, patients, settings, etc.) each bundling its own components, hooks, lib and data for a dental-practice domain area.

layers.name: [FE] Shared Libraries & State
layers.description: Cross-cutting client utilities — RBAC/guards, error-toast, logger, runtime config, the Zustand org-context store, shared hooks, domain constants, global styles and test utilities.

layers.name: [FE] Tooling & Scripts
layers.description: Journey-harness and stability-budget runner scripts that drive verification gates outside the app runtime.

layers.name: [FE] Configuration
layers.description: Build, lint, styling and runtime configuration (Vite, Tailwind, PostCSS, ESLint, Playwright, tsconfig, package manifest, env example, .dockerignore).

layers.name: [FE] Documentation
layers.description: Project README and contributor/component/screen reference docs for the dentalemon frontend.

layers.name: [FE] Infrastructure
layers.description: Container and edge-serving definitions — Dockerfiles, the Caddy reverse-proxy config, and the container entrypoint script.

layers.name: [BE] Application Entry
layers.description: Hono app bootstrap and process entrypoint (src/app.ts, src/index.ts) that wires middleware, routes, and starts the server.

layers.name: [BE] API Handlers & Routing
layers.description: Per-module Hono routers, generated route-binding shims, operationId handlers, request validators, and business facades under src/handlers that implement the nine domain modules.

layers.name: [BE] Data & Persistence
layers.description: Drizzle repositories (*.repo.ts), table schemas (*.schema.ts), erasure facades, DICOM/cephalometric processors and seed catalogs that own database access and the PostgreSQL data model.

layers.name: [BE] Shared Core Infrastructure
layers.description: Cross-cutting platform services in src/core — config, logger, errors, Better-Auth, tenant-tx/RLS, storage, audit, database, jobs scheduler, websocket, email, metrics, OpenAPI.

layers.name: [BE] HTTP Middleware
layers.description: Hono middleware for auth, request context, validation, dependency injection, response expansion, security headers, and metrics.

layers.name: [BE] Utilities & Shared Types
layers.description: Cross-cutting helpers (cors, date, query, expand, webrtc, auth) and shared TypeScript type definitions used across modules.

layers.name: [BE] Configuration
layers.description: Project and build configuration — package.json, tsconfig, bunfig, eslint, env example, cadence.yml, and the generated health OpenAPI document.

layers.name: [BE] Scripts & Tooling
layers.description: Code generation, database seeding, migration linting, RLS/boundary/registry checks, dev-doctor, and the Drizzle config — developer and CI tooling.

layers.name: [BE] Documentation
layers.description: Project and module reference docs (README, CONTRIBUTING, TESTING, and the per-feature guides under docs/).

layers.name: [BE] Deployment Infrastructure
layers.description: Container and dependency orchestration — Dockerfile and docker-compose dependency stacks for running the API and its backing services.

tour.title: [FE] Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand what dentalemon is: a React 19 + Vite single-page frontend for a dental practice, built on TanStack Router file-based routing, TanStack Query data hooks over the @monobase/sdk-ts client, Better-Auth, and Zustand. The README lays out the tech stack, quick-start, project structure, and route map — the roadmap for everything that follows in this tour.

tour.title: [FE] Application Bootstrap
tour.description: src/app.tsx is the runtime entry point mounted from index.html. It bootstraps runtime configuration, wires up OneSignal push and the PIN inactivity-session lifecycle, then renders the router. This file is your bird's-eye view of how the app comes alive before any screen is shown.

tour.title: [FE] Router & Route Tree
tour.description: Building on the bootstrap, src/router.tsx constructs the TanStack Router instance from the generated route tree, injecting the TanStack Query client into router context. routeTree.gen.ts is the codegen output that maps every file in src/routes/ to a typed route — never edited by hand. Together they define the navigation skeleton of the whole app.
tour.languageLesson: TanStack Router uses file-based routing: a route's path is derived from its filename, and routeTree.gen.ts is generated automatically. Underscore-prefixed segments like _dashboard create pathless layout routes that wrap children without adding a URL segment.

tour.title: [FE] Auth Guards & RBAC
tour.description: Routes are protected by composable beforeLoad guards in src/lib/guards.ts (auth, guest, person-presence, email-verification, role) combined via composeGuards. src/lib/rbac.ts holds the role matrix and predicates like canViewFinancials and canEditChart that gate clinical, billing, and admin capabilities. This is the security backbone every protected route relies on.
tour.languageLesson: TanStack Router beforeLoad runs before a route renders; throwing a redirect inside it blocks unauthorized navigation server-side of the render. Factory functions that return guards let you compose multiple checks declaratively per route.

tour.title: [FE] Org Context & Shared State
tour.description: src/stores/org-context.store.ts is the most depended-upon module in the app (highest fan-in): a Zustand store holding the active organization, branch ids, and role. load-org-context.ts hydrates it from the PIN session during route load, so guards and feature hooks everywhere read the current tenant and permissions from one place. This closes the loop between auth (Step 4) and the feature data layer ahead.
tour.languageLesson: Zustand stores are plain hooks with no provider; any component or non-React function (like a router guard) can read or update global state directly, which is why org context can be loaded during route resolution rather than only inside components.

tour.title: [FE] Route Layouts & App Shell
tour.description: Three pathless layout routes split the app by audience: _dashboard (staff admin: calendar, billing, patients, reports, settings), _workspace (the clinical chairside experience), and _portal (the patient-facing view). The app-sidebar frames staff screens. Each layout applies its own guards from Step 4 and renders an Outlet for its child routes.

tour.title: [FE] The Shared Data Layer
tour.description: Feature hooks fetch and mutate data through TanStack Query wrappers over the generated @monobase/sdk-ts client — use-visits.ts is a representative example, listing a patient's visits with ISO date normalization. error-toast.ts and logger.ts provide the cross-cutting failure-surfacing (with request-id refs) and structured logging that nearly every hook funnels through. Understanding this pattern unlocks every feature slice below.

tour.title: [FE] Clinical Workspace & Charting
tour.description: The clinical workspace ($patientId route) is the central chairside screen, orchestrating visit lifecycle, the interactive dental chart, treatment plans, and clinical sheets. The SVG dental-chart renders per-tooth and per-surface state with cumulative cross-visit layers (proposed/completed/declined). This is the largest and most important feature slice — the heart of the product.

tour.title: [FE] Periodontal Charting
tour.description: Building on the workspace, the perio module captures full periodontal exams: a probing-depth grid, BOP dots, classification panel, and a longitudinal comparison view that trends current-vs-history measurements. It even supports voice-driven entry. perio-types.ts is a high-fan-in shared contract underpinning all perio components.

tour.title: [FE] Imaging & Cephalometric Analysis
tour.description: The imaging slice manages radiographs, FMX mounts, CBCT studies, and image comparison, all built on the use-imaging-studies hook (notable fan-in). The cephalometric tracing overlay lets clinicians place landmarks and compute angle measurements for orthodontic analysis. This is the most geometry-heavy feature area in the codebase.

tour.title: [FE] Scheduling & Calendar
tour.description: The scheduling slice powers the staff calendar (day/week/month views), appointment booking modal, the live queue board, and recall-due lists. appointment-card is a reusable high-reuse building block across the calendar views. This is where the daily operational flow of the practice is driven.

tour.title: [FE] Billing & Collections
tour.description: The billing slice renders the invoice worklist, invoice detail with insurance blocks, payment plans, receipts, and a claims/collections workflow. Access is gated by the canViewFinancials predicate from Step 4's RBAC. format-currency.ts is a shared utility used across every money-rendering surface here.

tour.title: [FE] Patient Portal
tour.description: The patient-facing _portal layout (Step 6) hosts a much narrower experience: patients view their own upcoming appointments and outstanding bills via the use-my-portal hook. Contrasting this slim slice with the staff dashboard shows how the same data layer serves two very different audiences under different guards.

tour.title: [FE] Build & Configuration
tour.description: Vite drives the build and dev server, package.json declares the workspace dependencies (React 19, TanStack, Better-Auth, shadcn/Radix), and tailwind.config.ts plus globals.css define the design system. These config files set the boundaries within which all the feature code above is compiled and styled.

tour.title: [FE] Containerization & Serving
tour.description: The capstone: the two-stage Dockerfile builds the SPA in a Bun stage and serves the static dist from a Caddy alpine image. The Caddyfile serves the SPA behind a Kubernetes ingress with gzip and a runtime config.json endpoint, while docker-entrypoint.sh injects runtime configuration into the read-only filesystem at startup — connecting back to the runtime-config bootstrap from Step 2.
tour.languageLesson: Multi-stage Docker builds use separate FROM stages: the Bun builder carries the full toolchain to compile the Vite app, while the final Caddy stage copies only the static dist — yielding a small, dependency-free runtime image. Runtime config injection at entrypoint keeps one image deployable across environments.

tour.title: [BE] Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand what @monobase/api-ts is: the reference TypeScript implementation of a spec-first, polyglot healthcare API built on Bun, Hono, Drizzle, and PostgreSQL. It frames the nine vertical-neutral domain modules and the OpenAPI-as-source-of-truth philosophy that the rest of the tour builds on. Read it as the roadmap before we open any code.

tour.title: [BE] Application Bootstrap
tour.description: src/index.ts is the process entrypoint that boots the server, and src/app.ts's createApp wires the whole runtime together: middleware, the generated route registry, and the core services. This file has the highest fan-out in the codebase, so it is the single best map of every subsystem you are about to learn. Everything downstream is reached by following the imports out of here.
tour.languageLesson: Hono apps are composed by chaining `app.use()` middleware and `app.route()` mounts on a single instance. A factory like `createApp()` keeps wiring testable — tests can build an app with stubbed auth instead of the production one.

tour.title: [BE] Configuration & Database Core
tour.description: Two depth-1 dependencies underpin almost everything: src/core/config.ts parses environment variables into a typed, validated config object, and src/core/database.ts builds the Drizzle connection and runs migrations on boot. database.ts is the most depended-upon module in the project (fan-in of 516), since every repository imports the shared db handle from here. Understanding these two explains how the app is configured and how it talks to Postgres.

tour.title: [BE] Authentication & Shared Types
tour.description: src/core/auth.ts integrates Better-Auth — providers, plugins, sessions, and cookies — which is how every authenticated request is identified. src/types/app.ts (fan-in 388) and src/types/auth.ts (231) define the Hono context variables and auth-derived types that flow through every handler, forming the vocabulary the request pipeline speaks. These are the contracts between the framework and the business logic.

tour.title: [BE] The Request Pipeline: Router → Validator → Handler → Repo
tour.description: Now we meet the per-module pattern that all nine domains follow. A representative slice is the dental-visit module: createDentalVisit.ts is an operationId handler that opens a visit inside a tenant-scoped transaction, while visit.repo.ts is the Drizzle repository it delegates persistence to. Generated route-binding shims (from TypeSpec) narrow the validated context and call these handlers, so handlers receive already-validated input and focus purely on business logic.
tour.languageLesson: This codebase is API-first: TypeSpec compiles to OpenAPI, which generates the route shims and request validators. You implement only the operationId handler — never edit the generated files. The handler name matches the operationId in the spec.

tour.title: [BE] Multi-Tenant Isolation: RLS & Branch Access
tour.description: Healthcare data is multi-tenant, and isolation is enforced at two layers. src/core/tenant-tx.ts (withTenantTx) wraps DB work in a transaction that sets the Postgres RLS session scope to the caller's branch/org ids, so the database itself rejects cross-tenant reads. src/handlers/shared/assert-branch-access.ts (fan-in 116) is the application-layer guard every handler calls first to confirm the user has an active membership in the branch. Together they form the defense-in-depth that the visit handler from Step 5 relied on.
tour.languageLesson: PostgreSQL Row-Level Security (RLS) attaches policies to tables that filter rows by a session variable. Setting `SET LOCAL` inside a transaction scopes the tenant context to that transaction only, so a leaked connection can never serve another tenant's rows.

tour.title: [BE] Domain: Visits & Clinical Records
tour.description: Building on the visit lifecycle from Step 5, the clinical module hangs records off a visit. updateDentalVisit.ts implements the visit state machine (draft → active → sealed), and createAttachment.ts attaches a file to a visit with branch-role enforcement — illustrating how clinical artifacts are gated by both the visit's status and the caller's role. This is the heart of the EMR: a visit is the anchor every clinical record points to.

tour.title: [BE] Domain: Billing & the Stripe Webhook
tour.description: Billing turns a sealed visit into money. createDentalInvoice.ts builds an invoice from a visit (validating consent and org billing settings), payInvoice.ts creates a Stripe payment intent, and handleStripeWebhook.ts is the asynchronous counterpart — it verifies signatures, enforces idempotency via a processed-event ledger, and routes events like payment_intent.succeeded to mark invoices paid. Webhooks are how the source of truth (Stripe) reconciles back into our invoice state machine.
tour.languageLesson: Stripe webhooks must be idempotent: the same event can be delivered more than once. The handler records each processed event id in a ledger and short-circuits duplicates, so a retried `charge.succeeded` never double-applies a payment.

tour.title: [BE] Domain: Scheduling & Availability
tour.description: Scheduling decides when care happens. availability.service.ts is the core engine that resolves bookable providers and computes free slots by subtracting existing bookings, while createAppointment.ts is the staff handler that books a slot inside a tenant-scoped transaction after validating patient and branch access. The public-facing createOnlineBooking.ts shows the same engine reused behind a rate-limited, unauthenticated path for patient self-service.

tour.title: [BE] Domain: Perio, Imaging & FHIR Export
tour.description: These specialty modules show domain depth layered on the same pipeline. completePerioChart.ts finalizes a periodontal chart and applies the AAP/EFP 2017 staging algorithm; createImagingStudy.ts records radiographic studies (intraoral, pano, ceph, CBCT); and generatePMD.ts assembles a Patient Medical Document, with exportPatientCareRecord.ts emitting it as a standards-compliant FHIR R4 bundle. Each still rides through branch-access checks and tenant transactions you saw in Steps 5–6.

tour.title: [BE] Domain: Organizations & Branches
tour.description: The org module is the tenancy backbone everything else scopes to. DentalBranchManagement_create.ts creates a branch within an organization, and DentalMembershipManagement_create.ts grants a user a role in a branch — the exact membership that assert-branch-access from Step 6 checks on every request. Without an org, branch, and membership, none of the clinical or billing flows above can run.

tour.title: [BE] Cross-Cutting: Audit Logging & PHI Safety
tour.description: Compliance requires that every sensitive action is logged without leaking protected health information. src/core/audit-logger.ts (fan-in 100) is the central utility handlers call to persist compliance entries, and it routes metadata through src/core/audit-phi-sanitizer.ts, which deep-redacts PHI from field values before anything is written. This is why the handlers in earlier steps can log freely without risking a HIPAA violation in the log stream.

tour.title: [BE] Cross-Cutting: Jobs, Notifications & Storage
tour.description: Asynchronous work runs on a pg-boss scheduler in src/core/jobs.ts (cron, interval, and delayed jobs), which powers things like appointment reminders and recall scans. notification.repo.ts orchestrates multi-channel notifications (email/push) that those jobs fan out, and storage/uploadFile.ts issues presigned S3/MinIO upload URLs — the mechanism behind the clinical attachments from Step 7. These are the platform services every domain leans on.

tour.title: [BE] Cross-Cutting: Data Governance — Erasure, Retention & Legal Hold
tour.description: GDPR and records-retention obligations are handled by a dedicated governance layer. requestErasureHandler.ts opens a right-to-be-forgotten request, retention-engine.ts enforces data-retention rules with hard safety invariants, and legal-hold-service.ts can freeze a subject's data so erasure and retention cannot delete records under legal hold. This trio shows how competing compliance demands are reconciled safely across the domain modules.

tour.title: [BE] The Data Model, Migrations & Local Stack
tour.description: We close where the data lives. database.schema.ts provides the shared Drizzle column-set builders (base/syncable/versioned fields) that every table reuses, and visit.schema.ts is a concrete example defining the dental-visit table and its status enums; drizzle.config.ts points the migration toolchain at Postgres. docker-compose.deps.yml provisions the full local stack — Postgres, MinIO, Mailpit, and stripe-mock — so you can run every flow in this tour end to end on your machine.
tour.languageLesson: Drizzle is schema-first: TypeScript table definitions are the source of truth, and `drizzle-kit` diffs them to generate SQL migrations. Migrations run on server boot (see Step 3), and a schema-drift check compares applied migrations against the code to catch code/DB mismatches early.
